exercises.title: SOLID: Single Responsibility Refactoring
exercises.description: Refactor a 'God class' that violates Single Responsibility Principle into separate focused classes. The original `UserManager` does validation, hashing, storage, and email — split it into four classes. Verify each class has exactly one job and that the refactored code produces the same output.
exercises.expectedOutput:
[DB] Stored user: Alice
[Email] Welcome email sent to alice@example.com
Created user id=1, name=Alice
Validation error: Name too short
hints: UserValidator.validate: check name length, '@' in email, and password length. Raise ValueError for the first failure.
UserRepository.save: user['id'] = self._next_id; self._next_id += 1; self._store[user['id']] = user; print; return user
WelcomeEmailer.send: just print the welcome message.

exercises.title: SOLID: Open/Closed with Strategy Pattern
exercises.description: Implement the Open/Closed Principle using the Strategy pattern for a discount calculator. The system should support adding new discount types without modifying existing code. Implement: `PercentageDiscount`, `FlatDiscount`, and `BuyXGetYDiscount` as strategy classes.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Base total (9 × $30): $270.00

20% off                   Total: $216.00  (saved: $54.00)
$50 off total             Total: $220.00  (saved: $50.00)
Buy 2 Get 1 Free          Total: $180.00  (saved: $90.00)
hints: PercentageDiscount: total = price * quantity; return total * (1 - self.percent / 100)
FlatDiscount: return max(0, price * quantity - self.flat_amount)
BuyXGetYFree: sets_of_group = quantity // (self.buy_x + self.get_y_free); free_items = sets_of_group * self.get_y_free; paid = quantity - free_items; return paid * price
9 items, buy 2 get 1 free: 9//3=3 groups, 3 free items, 6 paid × $30 = $180.

exercises.title: Layered Architecture
exercises.description: Build a small application with 3 clean layers: Data Layer (in-memory storage), Business Logic Layer (validation + rules), and Presentation Layer (formatting output). Show that each layer only depends on the layer below it. Implement a simple inventory management system.
exercises.expectedOutput:
ID   Name                   Qty    Price
------------------------------------------
1    Widget Pro               3  $  29.99 ⚠
2    Gadget X                 3  $  89.99 ⚠
3    Tool Plus              100  $  14.99
4    Device Max               5  $ 199.99 ⚠

LOW STOCK ALERT: 3 items need restocking
  - Widget Pro: 3 remaining
  - Gadget X: 3 remaining
  - Device Max: 5 remaining
hints: add_item validation: if not name: raise ValueError('Name required'); if quantity < 0: raise...; if price <= 0: raise...
sell: item = self.repo.find_by_id(item_id); if item['quantity'] < quantity: raise ValueError('Insufficient stock')
low_stock_items: return [item for item in self.repo.find_all() if item['quantity'] <= self.LOW_STOCK_THRESHOLD]

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Class Inheritance
